reviews.review_title: This is GREAT in Mixed Reality!
reviews.review_description: This an excellent usage of the tech lets you experience the puzzle in real or virtual space. Love the puzzle designs so far and will be picking up the full game to see all the additional squingles. Weither you're looking for a fun challange via speedrunning through the levels or just want a relaxing puzzler to play in this one earns its spot on my recommendations.
Brilliant

Squingle has the right formula for what could become a classic game, they take a buzz game premise and expand on it, even in this mini version of the game from the tutorial levels to the first 10 actual levels, they guide you through several changes and it makes me eager to see what the full game will throw at me.
You have the option to play in MR or full immersion with the trippy psychedelic visuals as background, it's the type of game where you could throw on an album and just chill out as you guide your light through the courses.
I can see a full version of this being a major time sink hole, where you just throw it on to play a few levels and suddenly you get a low battery warning and a couple hours have gone by.
It's a very polished game and it looks like a lot of work has gone into it.

reviews.review_title: intro levels to Squingle, very fun
reviews.review_description: This includes 21 intro levels, which can be easily finished in 20 minutes, but improving your performance can take maybe an hour. Full game is 100 (harder) levels. Very well designed gameplay. Very polished. Very shiny. You can easily play for a few minutes at a time.

Fun demo of a simple puzzle concept. Takes about an hour to beat all the levels, but has a leaderboard for speedruns for anyone who likes to try and be fast. Puzzles themselves are good, good difficulty progression with the available sets. Even the intermissions are fun, with a yo-yo style interaction while cutscenes are going. Overall a great slice of a full game to try out before buying.

reviews.review_title: A Fun and Addictive Puzzle Game
reviews.review_description:
Squingle Mini is a unique puzzle experience that can be played in mixed reality in your own environment, or in virtual reality in its own immersive environments. You're guided along its levels via a relaxing voice over that teaches you the basics of manipulating a single orb, and then later revolving orbs, through eleven different 3D mazes.

The game is fun from the beginning, with simple starter levels that allow you to easily grasp the mechanics, but as you progress, the mazes become more challenging. With the obstacles and interactive switches that change the rotation of your orbs, you won't breeze through all the levels on your first try.

Once you complete the levels, that will unlock the leaderboard which will put your cumulative score up against others to see who can clear the mazes in the shortest time. If you are competitive and enjoy climbing the leaderboard, then this game will have you coming back to replay each level, trying to shave off tenths of a second of your time. As you are trying beat your previous times, you'll be racing against the ghost of your fastest times to help motivate you to be faster.

I've enjoyed my time with Squingle Mini and look forward to the full game. If you're looking for a fun MR/VR experience with unique 3D puzzle gameplay, then I would give Squingle Mini a try.

As of today, a big flex of mine is getting a perfect score on Squingle in all the 20 levels, plus the 1 very difficult bonus level! The puzzles are extremely well designed and probably took a while. The game is all about timing and waiting for the right time, but also having to make split second choices at times, it's all about timing in the puzzles. You start off fairly simple, with easy but fun puzzles. But at the later levels, you have fun along with the challenge of finishing the game, especially when you want a perfect score with all 3 clovers intact and all of the orbs collected. İn the game, you have to guide a ball to the end goal, sounds easy right? Well, that's certainly how it starts with. You can't push too far or else you'll lose a clover(basically a life). Then it starts getting more difficult, you suddenly have to guide two orbs who orbit around each other, so you have to heavily rely on timing, then you get different colored orbs that make your orbs orbiting change direction and you have to make some quick thinking to decide whether or not you want to get one, or else you'll lose a clover! Overall, the game is extremely fun and not too easy. Highly recommend and can't wait for the full game with a bunch more levels!

Squingle Mini is an absolutely mesmerizing VR puzzle game that offers a unique blend of challenge and charm. The surreal visuals draw you in, creating an immersive experience that feels both captivating and otherworldly. Guiding the revolving orbs through intricate 3D mazes is not only clever but also incredibly addictive, making it hard to put down. The progressive soundtrack enhances the journey, perfectly complementing the game's atmosphere.

What sets Squingle apart is its squidgy logic, which invites players to think outside the box while providing a satisfying sense of accomplishment with each solved puzzle. Competing with friends on the leaderboards adds an exciting layer of competitiveness, pushing you to improve your skills and race against your own ghost. The Mixed Reality update makes the experience even more personal and engaging.

With the highest user rating in the Quest store, it's clear that Squingle’s full game has resonated with players. The clever design and enchanting gameplay create a perfect balance of challenge and enjoyment. If you're looking for a VR game that combines puzzle-solving with a dreamy aesthetic, Squingle Mini is a must-play!

developer_response.body: "A must-play!" - Thank you so much MissChiefVR. Your review is magnificent, thank you!

reviews.review_description: The game is pretty cool if you like puzzle games, it's great if u just want to relax and listen to some chill music.
The game is easy, get to one point to another, but completing it perfectly is another matter. This is a maze puzzle game thats easy to start but difficult to master.

I really enjoy the Squingle it's a delightful puzzle game that captivates with its mesmerizing visuals and engaging gameplay. From the moment I started, the morphing, glass-like mazes drew me in. Navigating the orbs through these intricate pathways was both challenging and satisfying. The game perfectly balances difficulty and accessibility, making it enjoyable for all ages.

The controls are intuitive, allowing for smooth gameplay whether standing or seated. I appreciated how easily I could pick up the game for a quick session or get lost in it for an extended period. The Mixed Reality feature adds a unique touch feels fresh and exciting.

Competing on the global leaderboard was a highlight, motivating me to improve my times and climb the ranks. 

Squingle is not just a game; it's an experience that combines stunning visuals with clever puzzles. It’s perfect for anyone looking for a quick yet immersive gaming session. I wholeheartedly give it a 5 rating for its creativity, accessibility, and sheer fun factor.

If you're familiar with Kuru Kuru Kururin on the Gameboy Advance, then that's all you need to know whether you will enjoy Squingle or not......just imagine a VR version of that complete with that extra dimension VR gives you.

For those not familiar with the mentioned title, the game is a simple concept.
You control an orb that has to make it through a tubular maze as fast as possible and as cleanly as possible.
You have 3 petals (read lives), each time you touch the side of the tubular maze you lose a life (and be penalized with extra time added to your "speed run")
Lose 3 lives and you return to the start (or the last checkpoint you pass over)
The mazes are 3 dimensional so require you to have a steady hand to get through unscathed as the routes are often very narrow.
You can move and spin the mazes (or just physically move) to help you get a clearer viewpoint at any time as you rush to the exit.
Dotted throughout each level are several blobs which are just for completionists. Gathering them all and passing the level; without taking damage will earn you a Crown for the stage. Perhaps they will reward you with something in the full game?

After completing the initial 10 levels, Mini Squingle really begins!
Now instead of taking control of one orb, you are controlling two that rotate slowly around a central point.
There is far more nuance in controlling a pair of rotating orbs through a tight 3D maze!
Now you need to figure out if you have to wait for the orbs to reach a particular point in its rotation, to pass through a narrow tube.
Gradually as you work through the levels, more obstacles (moving) and mechanics are introduced.
In some levels, you have to pass each of the orbs through two tight parallel paths (making sure orbs are in the right rotation point to be able to pass through unscathed)
Other levels introduce "blue things" (literally what the game calls them XD); this swaps the direction of the orb rotation when touched.
Due to the way levels are laid out, you will have to rotate the orbs rotation to make it through certain tubes.
You also gain the ability to increase the speed the two orbs rotatation (essential for those looking to compete on time)
After the 10 "real" levels are finished you just have the bonus level which ramps up the difficulty dramatically (and introduces another mechanic that switches the axis the orbs rotate)
How much longer it will hold your attention will be dependent on how much you want to compete at the scoreboard.

A puzzle game that requires you to have a steady hand and a little dash of guts, as you push for a better times.

For another comparison, if you enjoyed the delicate controls of the Super Monkey Ball series of games then you will probably like this game.

Squingle scratches that same itch at the mentioned titles but this time accurately tracking your hand movement as your control method.

Competitive players will surely get more out of this slice of Sqingle. For others, it's a great way to sample a piece of the action and figure out if the game is for you.

I wonder what all the other mechanics the voice-over mentions will actually entail? (in the full game)

So far I would rate my experience 7/10. I played about 2 and a half hours on the quest 3 and mainly played in mixed reality. Also, I played up until the second to last level.

My favorite moment was getting 10/10 on almost every level. On the other hand my least favorite moment was when I went to punch the air in non-pass through mode and broke my chair.

The voiceover didn’t really explain much and I kinda just had to work out most of the game through trial and error. I wanted to mute the voiceover because it was kinda annoying but wasn’t able to. Although, if I could change anything, I would add a slow mode so that it’s easier to not hit a wall in the mazes.

I would say that the game play was driving me back to the game although that’s only part of the reason. I mainly just wanted to finish the playtest. Although, I did want to leave the game multiple times after falling 1 level over and over and over again.

I didn’t encounter any bugs that were to game breaking although there was one that registered as hitting a wall when I was nowhere near any.

Overall, it’s a good minigame for $0.99 and has great value although the full game seems like too high of a price and would be better at $15 instead of $19.99. I would say that playing the minigames does incentivize me to get the full game. The mixed reality is fun and I’d rate it 7/10 as an MR game. Unfortunately I dont think I’ll be getting the full game as I dont really like puzzles too much and the price is too high. 7/10 🧩.

At first the game didn't engage me enough because of how simple and easy the puzzles were. But after a certain point in the demo. The challenge picks up a bit.

The most enjoyable part for me was trying to finish the levels as fast as possible and beat my friends high score on the leaderboard

This game is a very simple concept that has some cool mixed reality levels. The goal is to guide two spheres that rotate from the beginning of a maze to the end without touching the sides while picking up items that increase your score on the way. The visuals are nice, colorful and kind of trippy. I enjoyed this so much before I knew it I had done about 15 levels. A must play for any puzzle game fans.

This is quite an engaging puzzle game. It reminds me a bit of the half life: alyx puzzles to turn on the upgrade machines. It has a bit of a speedrunning potential, and requires a bit of speed in that regard. The learning curve is okay, and the narrator telling us what to do is also nice. All in all a rather chill puzzle game.

In this game, you navigate a tubular, three-dimensional maze that can also be played in mixed reality. You can use your controller to change the maze's position in your space. The goal is to guide your orb from start to finish without touching the walls, while collecting dots scattered throughout the maze. As the game progresses, the levels become more complex, requiring “spatial” thinking. Additionally, there are multiple orbs spinning, checkpoints, and other orbs that can change the rotation of your own orb. At the end of each level, you get a score based on how many dots you collected and how little you touched the walls (represented by leaves). Touch the walls too much, and you lose one of your three leaves. Touch them even more, and you’ll go back to the start or your latest checkpoint. This is explained clearly with a voice guiding you at the start. For competitive players, there is a leaderboard that tracks your time and compares it to other players.

The game is really as simple as described above. This simplicity, along with the relaxing music, makes it one of the most relaxing games I have played recently. At the time of writing this review, I have completed the first 20 levels and the bonus one. I really hope the rest of the levels in the full version are as challenging —and even more so— than the bonus one.

However, I think this should be a trial version of the game so people are more incentivised to buy the full game.

Cheap way to see if you'd like the full game, this demo gives a solid hour or so of fun. Quite polished, didn't experience any bugs in my time with it, and a fun game to mini-speedrun. I would appreciate if there were individual level leaderboards though, rather than only a full game one, but that's not enough for me to take off a star, excellent stuff!

developer_response.body:
"a fun game to mini-speedrun"
Many thanks for the feedback roebro! I'm glad you enjoy Squingle Mini!

reviews.review_description:
Squingle mini is a unique experience where you use your hands to move a point of light (or two) through three-dimensional mazes in a psychadelic environment or mixed reality. It is somewhat reminiscent of those old toys, where you try to move a loop of wire along a winding path without touching the sides.
I have always been a fan of puzzle games, and games which involve physical skill - Squingle manages to blend the two very nicely.
The sound design is relaxing, for the most part, and the gameplay is straightforward and easy to understand.

Squingle Mini is a very short experience, with the bulk of playtime coming from replaying levels to aim for a better score. I believe a couple of changes need to be implemented in order to make the speedrunning/high-score hunting experience a bit smoother (such as being able to retry a level without resetting its position in space), but they do not take away from the overall experience.

In all, I can easily recommend Squingle as a relaxing puzzle experience. Just bear in mind that this "mini" version is essentially a demo, and has very limited content if you are not interested in speed or high scores.

This puzzle game serves as an excellent introduction to Squingle and offers a good amount of content. In Squingle, your objective is to guide small balls through various levels without making contact with the walls. The game provides approximately 1 to 2 hours of gameplay, depending on your skill level, and includes some bonus levels as well. You can enjoy the game in mixed reality (MR) mode at home or in virtual reality (VR) mode set in a peaceful galaxy. If you're a fan of puzzle games, then this game is perfect for you.

developer_response.body:
"Mini-Mazing! 🟣🟢"
Thanks flexfinity! Do join our Discord and channels for more updates!
